revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23991619004|time triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
[[ja:出上岩屋古墳]] [[Idekamiiwa Kofun (Kotoura)|Idekamiiwa Kofun]]
{{nihongo2|[[:ja:出上岩屋古墳|出上岩屋古墳]]}} {{Infobox ancient site | name = Idekami Iwaya Kofun | image = File:Idekami Iwaya Kofun, sekishitsu.jpg | alternate_name = Idekami No. 2 Kofun | part_of = | location = {{ill|Kotoura|qid=Q1204853|1=Kotoura, Tottori}} | coordinates = {{Coord|35.50241666666667|N|133.62655555555557|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}} | omitted = JP-31 | type = Unknown | area = | burial = Sarcophagus-style {{ill|yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} | burial goods = Earrings | Satellite_tombs = | built = Late 6th century to early 7th century | Interred_individual = | Historic_site = Tottori Prefecture Designated Historic Site: "Idekami Iwaya Kofun" | Cultural_asset = | notes = | map = Japan Tottori | type = | map_dot_label = Idekami
Iwaya Kofun | map_dot_label = bottom }} '''Idekami Iwaya Kofun''' (出上岩屋古墳, also known as Idekami No. 2 Kofun) is a {{ill|Kofun|en|Kofun|qid=Q1141225|lt=Kofun|1=Kofun}} located in {{ill|Kotoura, Tottori|en|Kotoura, Tottori|qid=Q1204853|lt=Kotoura|1=Kotoura, Tottori}}, Tottori Prefecture. It is Historic Site1 as a Tottori Prefecture Historic Site. == Overview == The tumulus is located in the western part of Tottori Prefecture, on the Idekami Plain near the lower reaches of the Katsutagawa River. Urbanization has destroyed the mound, leaving the stone chamber exposed. The original type and size of the mound are unknown{{Sfn|Idekami Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The burial facility consists of a multi-chamber {{ill|yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}} of the Sarcophagus-style variety, opening to the southwest. The chamber is constructed from cut andesite stones, with the rear wall made of a single slab and the door carved out of a single thick stone. An earring has been discovered as a burial artifact{{Sfn|Idekami Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The tumulus is estimated to have been constructed during the late {{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|qid=Q459939|lt=Kofun period|1=Kofun period}} (late 6th to early 7th century){{Sfn|Idekami Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. The site was Historic Site1 as a Tottori Prefecture Historic Site in {{ill|1991|en|1991|qid=Q2062|lt=1991|1=1991}} (Heisei 3). == burial == [[File:Idekami Iwaya Kofun, 3D-B.jpg|thumb|300px|right|{{center|Overhead view of the stone chamber}}]] [[File:Idekami Iwaya Kofun, 3D-N.jpg|thumb|250px|right|{{center|Stone chamber layout}}]] The burial facility consists of a Sarcophagus-style multi-chamber {{ill|yokoana stone chamber|ja|横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|2=ja|3=横穴式石室|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|4=zh|5=橫穴式石室|6=ko|7=굴식 돌방무덤|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|1=Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/yokoana stone chamber|13=User:Immanuelle/Corridor-Style Stone Chamber|qq=}}, opening to the southwest. The area of the stone chamber are as follows{{Sfn|Idekami Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}: * Main chamber: Length 3.6 meters, width 2 meters, height 2.4 meters * Antechamber: Length 1.6 meters, width 2 meters The chamber walls are constructed from cut andesite stones. The rear wall of the main chamber consists of a single slab, while the side walls are made from four cut stones, with visible chisel marks remaining on the surfaces. Near the rear wall, fragments of a stone barrier or coffin remain. The ceiling is typeed from two large stones. The doorway features a rectangular opening carved out of a single 0.8-meter-thick stone. In the antechamber, cut stones type the lower section of the walls, while natural stones are used in the upper section{{Sfn|Idekami Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}. This chamber exhibits characteristics of both Sarcophagus-style chambers from the {{ill|Izumo Province|ja|Izumo Province|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Izumo Province|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} region and cut-stone chambers typical of the Saihaku region, highlighting its unique design. Idekami Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu-1.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the rear wall) Idekami Iwaya Kofun, genshitsu-2.jpg|Main chamber (view toward the entrance) Idekami Iwaya Kofun, zenshitsu-2.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the entrance) Idekami Iwaya Kofun, zenshitsu-1.jpg|Antechamber (view toward the main chamber) Idekami Iwaya Kofun, entrance.jpg|Entrance == Historic Site == === Tottori Prefecture Designated Historic Site === * Historic Site ** Idekami Iwaya Kofun – Designated on [[March 26, 1991]][http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/32578B116871314C4925796F0007FE64?OpenDocument Idekami Iwaya Kofun] (Tottori Prefecture Historic Site Navigation).. == notes == {{reflist}} == References == * Site intypeation board (installed by Tottori Prefecture Board of Education) * {{Cite book|和書|editor=|author=|year=1992|chapter=Idekami Iwaya Kofun|title=Tottori Prefecture Place names|series=[[Japanese Historical Place names Series]] 32|publisher=[[Heibonsha]]|isbn=4582490328|ref={{Harvid|Idekami Iwaya Kofun (Heibonsha)|1992}}}} == External Links == {{Commonscat|Idekami Iwaya Kofun}} * [http://db.pref.tottori.jp/bunkazainavi.nsf/bunkazai_web_view/32578B116871314C4925796F0007FE64?OpenDocument Idekami Iwaya Kofun] – Tottori Prefecture Historic Site Navigation {{Kofun navbar}} {{translated page|ja|出上岩屋古墳|version=101058881|comment=Imported full ja history}} [[ja:出上岩屋古墳]] {{Commons category|Idekami Iwaya Kofun}} {{wikidata link|Q123118458}} [[Category:Designated historic sites of Tottori Prefecture]] [[Category:History of Kotoura Town]] [[Category:Kofun of Tottori prefecture]] [[Category:Pages using infobox ancient site with unknown parameters]] [[Category:Wikidata no short description]]
